legal_basis: Ley 24.012 (1991) and Electoral Code Art. 60 bis before parity reform
source_quote: Law 27.412 preamble states Law 24.012 had set a minimum 30% women on electoral lists; IDEA country page classifies Argentina under legislated candidate quotas with list non-compliance not accepted.
notes: Law 24.012 adopted in 1991 but took effect for candidate lists beginning with the 1993 legislative election cycle; interval closed in 2017 after parity law enactment.

legal_basis: Ley 27.412 (Paridad de Género), including alternation requirement and party-law sanctions
source_quote: Ley 27.412 requires lists be formed "ubicando de manera intercalada a mujeres y varones" and adds non-compliance sanction in party law; decree confirms implementation details.
notes: Law 27.412 published 15 December 2017 and implemented via Decree 171/2019; coded zipper because lists must alternate women and men.
